data.topic: github.event.inputs inputs context default values type coercion
data.content:
### Define Reusable Workflow Inputs with Type and Default Values

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Shows how to use on.workflow_call.inputs to define typed input parameters for a reusable workflow. Each input requires a type parameter (boolean, number, or string) and can have optional description, default value, and required flag. Inputs are accessed within the workflow using the inputs context.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      username:
        description: 'A username passed from the caller workflow'
        default: 'john-doe'
        required: false
        type: string

jobs:
  print-username:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Print the input name to STDOUT
        run: echo The username is ${{ inputs.username }}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Workflow Inputs for Manual Trigger (GitHub Actions YAML)

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/events-that-trigger-workflows

This snippet demonstrates how to define custom inputs such as 'logLevel', 'tags', and 'environment' for a GitHub Actions workflow that is manually triggered via `workflow_dispatch`. It specifies input properties like description, required status, default values, and data types (e.g., 'choice', 'boolean', 'environment'), then accesses these inputs within a job using the `inputs` context.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
        - info
        - warning
        - debug
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: false
        type: boolean
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  log-the-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo "Log level: $LEVEL"
          echo "Tags: $TAGS"
          echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        env:
          LEVEL: ${{ inputs.logLevel }}
          TAGS: ${{ inputs.tags }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}

```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions: Define Inputs for Manually Triggered Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/choosing-when-your-workflow-runs/triggering-a-workflow

This GitHub Actions workflow demonstrates how to define various input types (choice, boolean, string, environment) for a `workflow_dispatch` event, enabling users to provide parameters when manually triggering the workflow. It includes a job that consumes one of these inputs, `inputs.tags`, and prints its value, illustrating how to access user-provided data within the workflow. Note that inputs are available in both `inputs` and `github.event.inputs` contexts.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }} 
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }} 

```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions: Use Action for Secure Input Processing

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/using-githubs-security-features-to-secure-your-use-of-github-actions

This snippet demonstrates the recommended approach to process context values in GitHub Actions by passing them as arguments to a dedicated JavaScript action. This method inherently prevents injection attacks because the context value is not used to directly generate a shell script, enhancing workflow security.

```yaml
uses: fakeaction/checktitle@v3
with:
  title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}

```

--------------------------------

### Define GitHub Action Inputs in YAML

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/creating-actions/metadata-syntax-for-github-actions

This YAML snippet demonstrates how to declare input parameters for a GitHub Action metadata file. It specifies two inputs: 'num-octocats' (optional with a default value) and 'octocat-eye-color' (required without a default). These inputs allow workflow files to pass data to the action at runtime.

```yaml
inputs:
  num-octocats:
    description: 'Number of Octocats'
    required: false
    default: '1'
  octocat-eye-color:
    description: 'Eye color of the Octocats'
    required: true
```

--------------------------------

### Use inputs Context in Manually Triggered Workflow

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

Shows a workflow triggered by workflow_dispatch event that accepts user-provided inputs through the inputs context. Defines build_id and deploy_target as strings and perform_deploy as boolean, with conditional job execution based on the perform_deploy input value.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_id:
        required: true
        type: string
      deploy_target:
        required: true
        type: string
      perform_deploy:
        required: true
        type: boolean

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.perform_deploy }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy build to target
        run: echo "Deploying build:${{ inputs.build_id }} to target:${{ inputs.deploy_target }}"
```

--------------------------------

### Define Workflow Inputs with workflow_dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows

Configure custom input properties with types, default values, and required flags for the workflow_dispatch event. This example demonstrates defining logLevel (choice), tags (boolean), and environment (environment type) inputs that can be accessed via the inputs context during workflow execution.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
        - info
        - warning
        - debug
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: false
        type: boolean
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  log-the-inputs:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          echo "Log level: $LEVEL"
          echo "Tags: $TAGS"
          echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        env:
          LEVEL: ${{ inputs.logLevel }}
          TAGS: ${{ inputs.tags }}
          ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.environment }}
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Bash: Process Input via Intermediate Environment Variable

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/using-githubs-security-features-to-secure-your-use-of-github-actions

This example shows how to handle untrusted input in inline scripts within GitHub Actions by storing the expression value in an intermediate environment variable. It processes the `github.event.pull_request.title` using Bash, effectively preventing script injection by isolating the input from the script's execution context. The value is stored in memory and used as a variable, not interacting with script generation.

```yaml
      - name: Check PR title
        env:
          TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
        run: |
          if [[ "$TITLE" =~ ^octocat ]]; then
          echo "PR title starts with 'octocat'"
          exit 0
          else
          echo "PR title did not start with 'octocat'"
          exit 1
          fi

```

--------------------------------

### Define Manual Workflow Inputs with workflow_dispatch Event

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Configure on.workflow_dispatch.inputs to accept manual parameters when triggering a workflow. Supports multiple input types including choice, boolean, string, and environment. Inputs are accessible via the inputs context and have a maximum payload of 65,535 characters with up to 25 top-level properties.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

--------------------------------

### Inputs Context for Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/accessing-contextual-information-about-workflow-runs

The inputs context contains input properties passed to an action, reusable workflow, or manually triggered workflow. Input names and types are defined in the workflow configuration and accessed during workflow execution.

```APIDOC
## Inputs Context

### Description
The `inputs` context contains input properties passed to an action, reusable workflow, or manually triggered workflow. For reusable workflows, inputs are defined in the `workflow_call` event configuration. For manually triggered workflows, inputs are defined in the `workflow_dispatch` event configuration.

### Context Properties
- **inputs** (object) - Required - Container for all input values passed to the workflow
- **inputs.<name>** (string | number | boolean | choice) - Required - Individual input value passed from an external workflow or workflow_dispatch event

### Availability
- Available in reusable workflows called via `workflow_call`
- Available in workflows triggered by `workflow_dispatch` event
- Accessible from any job or step in a workflow

### Usage Pattern
Access input values using: `${{ inputs.<name> }}`

### Property Types
Each input value can be one of the following types:
- **string** - Text input value
- **number** - Numeric input value
- **boolean** - True/False input value
- **choice** - Selection from predefined options

### Configuration Example
Inputs are defined in the workflow file under `workflow_dispatch` or `workflow_call` event configuration and are passed from external workflows or manual triggers.
```

--------------------------------

### Pass Inputs to Reusable Workflows in GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Provide input values to called workflows using the `with` keyword. Inputs must match input specifications defined in the called workflow and are accessible via the `inputs` context rather than as environment variables.

```yaml
jobs:
  call-workflow:
    uses: octo-org/example-repo/.github/workflows/called-workflow.yml@main
    with:
      username: mona
```

--------------------------------

### on.workflow_call.inputs Configuration

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Specifies the input parameters that a reusable workflow accepts from a caller workflow. These inputs can have descriptions, default values, and a defined type.

```APIDOC
## `on.workflow_call.inputs`

### Description
When defining a reusable workflow using `on.workflow_call`, this section allows you to define the inputs that can be passed from the caller workflow. These inputs become available within the called workflow via the `inputs` context.

### Usage
As a sub-property of `on.workflow_call`.

### Fields
- **<input_id>** (object) - Required - A unique identifier for the input parameter.
  - **description** (string) - Optional - A brief description of the input.
  - **default** (string | number | boolean) - Optional - The default value for the input if not provided by the caller.
    - Default values: `false` for boolean, `0` for number, `""` for string if not set.
  - **required** (boolean) - Optional - Specifies if the input must be provided by the caller workflow. Defaults to `false`.
  - **type** (string) - Required - The data type of the input. Must be one of `boolean`, `number`, or `string`. See `on.workflow_call.inputs.<input_id>.type`.

### Configuration Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      username:
        description: 'A username passed from the caller workflow'
        default: 'john-doe'
        required: false
        type: string

jobs:
  print-username:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Print the input name to STDOUT
        run: echo The username is ${{ inputs.username }}

```

### Notes
Inputs not specified in the called workflow, but passed by a caller workflow, will cause an error.
```

--------------------------------

### Use Custom Action Instead of Inline Script - GitHub Actions

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/secure-use-reference

Creates a secure GitHub Actions workflow by delegating input processing to a custom action rather than using inline scripts. This approach prevents script injection attacks because the context value is passed as an argument to the action instead of being interpolated into shell script generation.

```yaml
uses: fakeaction/checktitle@v3
with:
  title: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Untrusted Input via Environment Variable - Bash Script

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/secure-use-reference

Demonstrates a secure approach to handle untrusted input in GitHub Actions inline scripts by storing the context value in an intermediate environment variable before use. This prevents script injection by avoiding direct interpolation into the shell script generation process. The example validates a pull request title using a Bash conditional statement.

```yaml
      - name: Check PR title
        env:
          TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
        run: |
          if [[ "$TITLE" =~ ^octocat ]]; then
          echo "PR title starts with 'octocat'"
          exit 0
          else
          echo "PR title did not start with 'octocat'"
          exit 1
          fi
```

--------------------------------

### Define Inputs for Manual Workflow Dispatch

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/trigger-a-workflow

Configure the `workflow_dispatch` event with typed input parameters including choice, boolean, string, and environment types. Inputs are passed to the workflow and accessible via the `inputs` context. Maximum 25 top-level properties and 65,535 character payload limit apply.

```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

--------------------------------

### Example inputs Context JSON Structure

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

Shows the JSON structure of the inputs context containing sample values for build_id, deploy_target, and perform_deploy inputs. This represents the data structure available when inputs are defined in a workflow.

```json
{
  "build_id": 123456768,
  "deploy_target": "deployment_sys_1a",
  "perform_deploy": true
}
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow Dispatch Inputs

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions

Defines custom input parameters for workflows triggered manually using the `workflow_dispatch` event. These inputs can be accessed in the workflow via the `inputs` context.

```APIDOC
## CONFIGURATION on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Description
Configures input fields for a workflow that can be triggered manually via `workflow_dispatch`. These inputs are passed to the workflow and are available in the `inputs` context. The maximum number of top-level properties for `inputs` is 25, and the maximum payload is 65,535 characters.

### Method
Configuration

### Endpoint
on.workflow_dispatch.inputs

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **<input_id>** (object) - Required - A string identifier for the input.
  - **description** (string) - Required - A brief description of the input.
  - **required** (boolean) - Required - Specifies whether the input is mandatory.
  - **default** (string) - Optional - The default value for the input if not provided.
  - **type** (string) - Required - The type of input. Supported types include `string`, `boolean`, `choice`, `environment`.
  - **options** (array of strings) - Optional (for `choice` type) - A list of allowed string values if `type` is `choice`.

### Request Example
```yaml
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      logLevel:
        description: 'Log level'
        required: true
        default: 'warning'
        type: choice
        options:
          - info
          - warning
          - debug
      print_tags:
        description: 'True to print to STDOUT'
        required: true
        type: boolean
      tags:
        description: 'Test scenario tags'
        required: true
        type: string
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to run tests against'
        type: environment
        required: true

jobs:
  print-tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.print_tags }}
    steps:
      - name: Print the input tag to STDOUT
        run: echo  The tags are ${{ inputs.tags }}
```

### Response
N/A
```

--------------------------------

### Use inputs Context in Reusable Workflow

Source: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts

Demonstrates a reusable workflow that defines and accesses inputs through the inputs context. The workflow accepts build_id (number), deploy_target (string), and perform_deploy (boolean) inputs, then uses them in a conditional job and echo command to deploy a build to a target system.

```yaml
name: Reusable deploy workflow
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      build_id:
        required: true
        type: number
      deploy_target:
        required: true
        type: string
      perform_deploy:
        required: true
        type: boolean

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ inputs.perform_deploy }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy build to target
        run: echo "Deploying build:${{ inputs.build_id }} to target:${{ inputs.deploy_target }}"
```
